trade.summary: Russia-Sudafrica trade has grown since 2022 as Sudafrica has not joined Western sanctions. Russia exports fertilizers (critical for Sudafrican agriculture), wheat, energy products, and nuclear technology services. Sudafrica exports minerals, agricultural goods, and manufactured products to Russia. Russia's fertilizer exports to Africa, including Sudafrica, have been positioned by Moscow as evidence of its commitment to African food security.

diplomatic.status: friendly — South Africa has maintained non-aligned stance favorable to Russia
diplomatic.summary: Russia-Sudafrica relations have become strategically significant since 2022. Sudafrica refused to condemn Russia's invasion of Ucraina, abstained on key UNGA resolutions, declined to arrest Putin when he was invited to the BRICS summit (opting for virtual participation), and conducted joint naval exercises with Russia and Cina. Russia cultivates Sudafrica as a leader of the 'Global South' non-aligned bloc. The ANC's historic ties with the Soviet Union (which supported the liberation struggle) provide ideological grounding.
recent_incidents: Sudafrica declined to arrest Putin for BRICS 2023 (ICC warrant)
Sudafrica conducted joint MOSI naval exercises with Russia and Cina (Feb 2023)
Sudafrica sent arms to Russia (alleged covert delivery — strongly denied by SA)
Russia's foreign minister Lavrov visited Sudafrica and Pretoria hosted peace talks (2023)

military.summary: Military relationship is developing. Sudafrica conducted joint naval exercises with Russia and Cina in February 2023, generating significant Western concern. Sudafrica has historic military ties with Russia through Soviet-era weapons systems (legacy equipment). Russia has offered military technology cooperation. Sudafrica was accused by the Stati Uniti of covertly supplying arms to Russia — allegations denied by Sudafrica but that damaged the relationship with Western partners.
military.alliance_status: non-aligned with de facto positive posture toward Russia
incidents: MOSI joint naval exercises (South Africa, Russia, China) — February 2023
US allegations of covert South African arms deliveries to Russia (2023)

financial.summary: Sudafrica has not imposed financial sanctions on Russia. Some Russian capital has sought Sudafrican channels. Rosatom is engaged in discussions on Sudafrica's nuclear expansion. Sudafrican commodity companies sell to Russian markets. BRICS financial cooperation (including New Development Bank, BRICS currency discussions) provides a framework for non-dollar financial dealings.

energy.summary: Rosatom has been in discussions with Sudafrica about building new nuclear power plants (Koeberg expansion and potential new nuclear capacity). Given Sudafrica's severe electricity shortage, this is a strategically important engagement. Russia has also offered energy technology cooperation. Sudafrica imports some Russian coal and energy products.

scientific.summary: Some scientific collaboration continues given Sudafrica's non-aligned status. Sudafrican and Russian universities maintain some partnerships. Nuclear science cooperation through Rosatom is significant. The SKA (Square Kilometre Array) telescope project in Sudafrica attracts some Russian participation.
